IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: FH Association is an organization of Christian motivation committed to helping the poor.
IRS990.MissionDesc: FH Association is an organization of Christian motivation committed to helping the poor and needy throughout more than 24 countries in the world by doing disaster/emergency relief and development programs.
IRS990.Desc: Food security and livelihoodSeed fairs, seed and tool distributions, technology enhancement to increase agricultural production, seed multiplication, forestry promotion, soil and water conservation.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: HEALTH PROGRAMS-Malnutrition and illness prevention, trainings on proper sanitation practices, health assessments, training and educating of local healthcare representatives, assistance with health insurance provision.HIV/AIDS-Clinical treatment, home-based care, education on proper nutrition and prevention, Biblical training on abstinence and faithfulness, medical support, outreach.

ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp.Desc: RELIEF & REHABILITATION PROGRAMS- Emergency relief for droughts, floods and other natural disasters, food distribution, water sanitation activities, health surveys, emergency shelter construction, road and bridge rehabilitation.

ProgSrvcAccomActyOtherGrp.Desc: CHILD DEVELOPMENT/EDUCATION & INTEGRATED DEVELOPMENT PROGRAMS- CHILD DEVELOPMENT-Parental counseling, medical checkups, education, school supplies, new skills development, HIV/AIDS awareness trainings, school building rehabilitation.CHILD PROTECTION-Psycho-social care and rehabilitation for traumatized children, advocacy for child-headed households, promotion of children's rights.
WATER & SANITATION- Irrigation and safe water well construction, hand dug wells, latrine construction, hygiene/sanitation education, water purification, biosand filter for household.
OTHER PROGRAMS- Church leadership development, leadership training and others.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The primary purpose of the organization's endowment funds is to provide child development program students with vocational training or secondary schooling scholarship assistance. The vision is for them to complete a degree of education that will adequately allow them to develop their capacities and prepare them to fulfill their role and purpose in their family and community, finishing with the tools they need to live a good life.

AccountActivitiesOutsideUSGrp.TypeOfActivitiesConductedTxt: Grants to Recipients Located in Region
Grants to Recipients Located in Region

Grants to Recipients Located in Region

GrantsToOrgOutsideUSGrp.PurposeOfGrantTxt: Trainings for intervention, evaluation and laboratory team
Best farming practices, Agroforestry, Animal health
Rehabilitation of school infrastructure and teachers incentive
Rehabilitation of school infrastructure and teachers incentive

ForeignIndividualsGrantsGrp.MannerOfCashDisbursementTxt: Bank wire transfers and cash given to beneficiaries
ForeignIndividualsGrantsGrp.DescriptionOfNonCashAsstTxt: Various commodities including but not limited to food and medical supplies

ForeignIndividualsGrantsGrp.DescriptionOfNonCashAsstTxt: Various commodities including but not limited to food and medical supplies

ForeignIndividualsGrantsGrp.MannerOfCashDisbursementTxt: Bank wire transfers and cash given to beneficiaries
ForeignIndividualsGrantsGrp.DescriptionOfNonCashAsstTxt: Various commodities including but not limited to food and medical supplies

ForeignIndividualsGrantsGrp.MannerOfCashDisbursementTxt: Bank wire transfers and cash given to beneficiaries
ForeignIndividualsGrantsGrp.DescriptionOfNonCashAsstTxt: Various commodities including but not limited to food and medical supplies

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: FH Association monitors program services through regional offices in Asia, Africa and Latin America and each field's headquarters office. The fields and regional offices submit a monthly financial report to the International headquarters office and all financial records are stored in its central server. We also have monitoring and evaluation personnel who visit projects.
Expenses are accounted for according to the accrual basis of accounting using grant feedback, expense reports, and other appropriate documentation.
FH Association grants funds to Food for the Hungry, Inc., a related organization. The use of funds are monitored by common management.

FH Association's U.S. affiliate, Food for the Hungry, Inc., determines officer compensation by looking at the 990s of other organizations and by reviewing compensation surveys. The final recommendation is approved by the Board of Directors.
The number of contributions reported is the number of contributions made, not the number of items contributed.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part I, Line 5, Part V, Line 2a, & Part IX, Lines 7-10:
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Most salary payments go to non-United States citizens, who are exempt from W-2/W-3 requirements. All United States citizen staff are compensated by a related organization, Food for the Hungry Inc. Food for the Hungry Inc. files all required payroll information returns as required by the IRS. As such, due to common paymaster rules as instructed by the IRS, the number of employees reported represent the number of U.S. employees who work for the filing organization but are compensated by a related organization. The amounts included in 990, Part IX, Lines 7-10 include the salaries and wages for both U.S. and foreign individuals.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section A, line 1
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The Executive Committee of the board is comprised of two persons reflecting different constituencies of the whole board. All persons on the Executive Committee are members of the full board. No staff members are members of the Executive Committee. The Executive Committee may exercise the full authority of the board except for actions requiring a majority vote by the full board under California law, actions concerning board or committee membership, changes to the governing documents, any approval of self-dealing transactions, or changes to compensation of top staff or directors.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, line 11b
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The Form 990 was prepared by an independent CPA firm and was reviewed in detail by the Chief Financial Officer and Director of Finance to verify accuracy. The 990 was then emailed to the board of directors prior to being filed with the IRS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, line 12c
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The conflict of interest policy covers top management and members of the board of directors. Individuals are required to sign conflict of interest statements on an annual basis. The corporate secretary reviews the signed statements and monitors transactions for potential conflicts of interest. Should any potential conflicts of interest be disclosed, the board member or officer would be asked to refrain from participation in any deliberation or decision with regard to matters affected by the relationship.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, line 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: FH Association does not compensate officers directly. Compensation is paid by Food for the Hungry, Inc., a related organization listed in Schedule R. The board of directors for Food for the Hungry, Inc. is responsible for determining compensation for the organization's officers. When determining an appropriate level of compensation for a given position, the board works closely with the human resources department. Comparability data is collected on salary and benefits offered by similar-sized, like-minded non-profit organizations. This information is used to set a benchmark in determining appropriate compensation to offer. The compensation policy is used to set compensation when an officer is first hired, as well as any time their compensation package is subject to change. All compensation decisions are recorded in documentation held by the Human Resources department. The board approves salaries and benefits in total when it approves the annual budget at its September meeting.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section C, line 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Food for the Hungry, Inc., a related organization of FH Association, posts FH Association's annual audits on its website at http://fh.org/about/finances. Copies of FH Assocation's tax returns, governing documents, and conflict of interest policy are available to the public upon request.
Unrealized gain on foreign currency exchange -53,794.

DisregardedEntityName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Food for the Hungry Foundation Inc
